assertion_results.text: Step 0 detects the absence of the Deployment Context column in the Source Repositories table and defaults all repositories to upstream (§1.78)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md line 34: 'The Source Repositories table in CLAUDE.md does **not** have a Deployment Context column. Per backward compatibility rules, all repositories default to `upstream`.' Also line 49: 'No Deployment Context column detected in Source Repositories table -- defaulting all repos to `upstream`.' Both statements explicitly detect the absence and apply the upstream default.

assertion_results.text: Remediation task descriptions do NOT include a Coordination Guidance subsection — the section is omitted entirely when no Deployment Context column exists
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md line 9: 'No Coordination Guidance subsection is included because the Source Repositories table does not have a Deployment Context column (backward compatibility).' All four task descriptions (Tasks 1-4) were inspected and none contain a Coordination Guidance subsection. Line 239 further confirms: 'No Coordination Guidance subsection is included in any task because the Source Repositories table in CLAUDE.md does not have a Deployment Context column. Per backward compatibility rules, this subsection is omitted entirely rather than defaulting to any deployment context content.'

assertion_results.text: The triage proceeds normally through all steps without errors from the missing Deployment Context column
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md completes Step 0 (Configuration Validation) and Step 1 (Data Extraction) with full version impact analysis across both 2.2.x and 2.1.x streams. remediation.md completes Step 8 (Remediation) producing 4 structured tasks with proper Jira linkage. No error messages, failure indicators, or incomplete steps appear in either output file.

assertion_results.text: Backward compatibility is maintained — existing eval behavior is unaffected by the absence of the Deployment Context column
assertion_results.evidence: Both files explicitly cite backward compatibility rules: data-extraction.md line 34 ('Per backward compatibility rules, all repositories default to upstream') and remediation.md line 239 ('Per backward compatibility rules, this subsection is omitted entirely'). The triage output follows standard structure with CVE parsing, version impact analysis, and remediation task generation with proper labels, descriptions, acceptance criteria, and Jira linkage — all consistent with expected eval behavior.
